channel.title: Llms on Shane Caldwell
channel.description: Recent content in Llms on Shane Caldwell

item.title: No Autonomy Without Scalable Oversight
item.description: What to expect as we enter the Year of The Judge

item.title: ProofJudge: Can we align vibe-proving with human taste?
item.description: Towards measuring alignment with human taste in autoformalization with judge agents.

item.title: The Tests All Pass
item.description: METR's SWE-bench analysis shows us taste isn't verifiable.

item.title: Intro to GPUs For the Researcher
item.description: Getting comfortable with the hardware on a quest for more MFU.

item.title: All Reduce Across the Atlantic: Bandwidth in Decentralized Training
item.description: The practical realities of devestatingly high communication cost in training.

item.title: Twenty Billion Tokens of What, Exactly?
item.description: Looking at the data and letting it look back at us.

item.title: Pretraining at home: 20B tokens from 222 hours to 12
item.description: Optimizing training a Llama 3.2 1B model so we can pretrain in a day without going broke.

item.title: Offsec Evals: Growing Up In The Dark Forest
item.description: If you contribute a public benchmark, are you giving free capability to your competitors?

item.title: DiLoCo: Data Parallelism for the Datacenter Poor
RL Needed LLMs Because Agency Requires Priors
item.description: We tried RL once. It didn't work. I'm confident it will this time.

item.title: GPT-5 is Good, Actually: The Agony and Ecstasy of Public Benchmarks
item.description: An attempt to explain why benchmarks are either bad or secret, and why the bar charts don't matter so much.

item.title: The Input Sanitization Perspective on Prompt Injection
item.description: An analysis of prompt injection vulnerabilities in large language models and why they represent a fundamental security challenge.
